participants.reasoning: The extracted participant description matches the official criteria and sample characteristics, including setting, age, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and total number enrolled.
participants.extracted: Community-dwelling adults aged 70 years or older in the southeast of the Netherlands (Maastricht, Sittard-Geleen, and Heerlen) who reported at least some concerns about falling, at least some related avoidance of activity, and fair or poor self-rated general health. Participants had to be willing to take part and provide informed consent. Exclusion criteria included being confined to bed, permanent wheelchair use, pending nursing home admission, substantial hearing or vision impairment, or cognitive impairment on screening (AMT4 and, if needed, TICS). To avoid reciprocal influence, only one member of a couple could participate. The trial ultimately included 389 participants.

Country: the Netherlands
n = 389 (intervention: 194, control: 195), at‐home sessions

Mean age (years): control: 78.25 (SD 5.3), intervention: 78.35 (SD 5.4)
Inclusion criteria: at least sometimes concerned about falls and associated activity avoidance, and valued their perceived general health as fair or poor assessed with 1 item of the Medical Outcomes Study‐20 (MOS‐20), and signed the informed consent
Exclusion criteria: confined to bed; wheelchair dependent; waiting for nursing

interventions.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official intervention’s home-based 7-session AMB format, themes, nurse delivery, and inclusion of an in vivo activity exposure with action planning, with only minor added detail about techniques that do not contradict the record.
interventions.extracted: The intervention was AMB-Home (A Matter of Balance at Home), an individual in-home cognitive behavioural programme for community-dwelling older adults with concerns about falling and activity avoidance. It consisted of seven sessions: three home visits and four telephone contacts. The programme aimed to reduce fear of falling and avoidance by using cognitive restructuring, motivational interviewing, action planning, modelling via a DVD, problem-solving, and gradual practice of personally relevant feared activities. Session themes included concerns about falls, thoughts about falling, physical exercise, asserting oneself, overcoming personal barriers, safe behaviour, and managing concerns about falls. Unlike the group version (AMB-NL), the home programme did not include supervised physical exercises; instead, it placed more emphasis on action plans and practising a fear-related activity in daily life during one home visit. Participants were also encouraged to involve a significant other, and the programme was delivered by trained community nurses.
interventions.official:
CBT with exercise
Arm 1: home‐based format of AMB; 7 individual sessions, including 3 home visits and 4 telephone contacts
Each session had a predefined theme; concerns about falls; thoughts about falling; physical exercise; asserting oneself; overcoming personal barriers; safe behaviour; managing concerns about falls.
Each session was similarly structured; review of the previous session (except the first session), a discussion of the main theme, the formulation of a personalised action plan rel

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes match the official outcomes, including FES-I, FES-IAB, GARS, and number of falls recorded via a fall diary/calendar.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure primary outcomes of concerns about falls using the 16-item Falls Efficacy Scale-International (FES-I; score range 16 to 64) and avoidance of activity owing to concerns about falls using the FES-I Avoidance Behaviour version (FES-IAB; 16 items, score range 16 to 64), assessed by telephone interview at baseline and at both follow-up time points (FU1 at 5 months and FU2 at 12 months). Secondary outcomes included disability in activities of daily life measured with the Groningen Activity Restriction Scale (GARS; 18 items, score range 18 to 72), assessed at baseline and both follow-ups, and number of falls, recorded continuously with a monthly fall calendar from baseline through the follow-up period and summarized at follow-up assessments.
outcomes.official: FES‐I; FES‐IAB; GARS; number of falls (fall diary)
